source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: SpaceX is the highest risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio) asset available in any market, public or private
summary_extended: Baker argues that SpaceX represents the highest risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio) of any asset available in public or private markets, making it his single highest-conviction investment idea. His reasoning rests on three pillars: Starlink alone addresses a $1 trillion-plus global telecom market with a product that is structurally superior to terrestrial alternatives, SpaceX is the most critical Western defense asset (surpassing even Anduril in his view), and the company carries long-term optionality he likens to becoming the 'British East India Company of the solar system.' Baker further notes that SpaceX is 'pro-entropic,' meaning its value increases as global disorder and geopolitical instability rise, providing a hedge against macro deterioration. The investment implication is a strong long position in SpaceX, though the company remains private and is not directly accessible via US-listed equities.
detail: Baker calls SpaceX his highest-conviction investment idea on a risk-adjusted basis. Starlink alone addresses a $1T+ telecom market with a uniquely superior product. SpaceX is also the most important Western defense asset (more than Anduril). Long-term optionality includes becoming the 'British East India Company of the solar system.' Pro-entropic: gets more valuable as world disorder increases.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly calls it 'the highest Sharpe ratio asset you can buy' — his strongest single investment statement in the transcript
quote_fragment: SpaceX is probably the highest risk-adjusted return available in the markets
